expectations.text: model_created: Script creates a model with model create command
part_created: A rigid body part is created with correct syntax
expectations.evidence: 'part create rigid_body name_and_position & part_name = .pendulum.link & location = ... & orientation = ...'

expectations.text: markers_created: At least a pin marker is created on the link and ground
expectations.evidence: pivot_mkr on ground, and pin_mkr, cm, tip_mkr, rod_mkr on .pendulum.link

expectations.text: revolute_joint: A revolute joint is created connecting the link to ground using i_marker_name and j_marker_name
gravity: Gravity field is created with -Y component (e.g. -9806.65 or -9.80665)
expectations.evidence: 'y_component_gravity = -9806.65' in force create body gravitational block

expectations.text: initial_angle: Part or marker orientation set to 45 degrees for initial release
full_keyword_names: All keywords spelled out fully (not abbreviated)
expectations.evidence: All commands use full keywords: model_name, part_name, marker_name, joint_name, i_marker_name, j_marker_name, gravity_field_name, center_of_mass_marker, etc.

expectations.text: dot_path_names: Object names use dot-path hierarchy `.model.part.marker`
expectations.evidence: All names use full dot-paths: .pendulum.ground.pivot_mkr, .pendulum.link, .pendulum.link.cm, .pendulum.link.pin_mkr, etc.

expectations.text: no_adams_id: No adams_id specified anywhere in the script
expectations.evidence: No occurrences of 'adams_id' found in the file

expectations.text: mass_properties_uses_modify: Mass properties set with part modify (not part create) since the part already exists
expectations.evidence: 'part modify rigid_body mass_properties & part_name = .pendulum.link & mass = 1.0 ...' used (not part create)

expectations.text: geometry_present: At least one geometry shape (sphere, cylinder, or box) is added to each moving part for visual inspection in Adams View
expectations.evidence: Cylinder rod (rod_cyl) and two ellipsoids (tip_bob, pin_ellips) added to .pendulum.link

expectations.text: adams_view_no_errors: CMD script runs in Adams View without any ERROR: lines in aview.log (check the log file)
expectations.evidence: run_adams_cmd.py exited 0; no 'ERROR:' lines found in aview.log (only benign matches like 'Error Max', 'Integration error =' simulation tolerance, and Python hook errors unrelated to the model)
